# Phase 10.0.2 Archive Integrity Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define how civilization proves preserved governance history remains authentic, untampered, and historically legitimate across extreme long horizons.

## Scope

- Constitutional archive integrity doctrine.
- Tamper-evidence governance doctrine.
- Historical authenticity verification standards.
- Archive trustworthiness doctrine.
- Anti-historical-rewrite doctrine.
- Governance archive corruption detection doctrine.
- Preservation integrity verification lineage.
- Constitutional preservation verification chains.
- Civilization archive trust architecture.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No archive integrity system creates runtime authority.
- No authenticity verification bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No preservation trust layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No archive trust framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows governance freeze enforcement and preservation architecture.
- Safe to include now: tamper-evidence doctrine, corruption detection, authenticity standards, trust states, lineage, and verification chains.
- Future upgrade: archive manifest and doctrine index certification.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
